msgs.text:
I treated the prompt and the 49-turn log as a proposal record, not as a proof. The prompt fixes the submission format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves one serious unrefuted candidate, the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď49; the earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants are refuted there by explicit finite-quotient tests.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ő∑ is immaterial in this word, since ŌĄ has prime-to-2 finite tame images.

1. Stable correction: two quotient formulas The recent turns repeatedly oscillate on two reciprocal formulas. I will fix the conventions unambiguously.For the sign-cover calculation, let V=kerőļ, let t be the deck involution, and restrict to a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

The geometric-series identity is (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ.Since ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on an e-th tame-character block,(tőĪa)ŌÄ=t.

Therefore the correct formula isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the only direction that supports the unit/radical argument: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit for őĪaÓÄ†=1, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient fromx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Thus nontrivial odd tame-character blocks still contribute only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Writing f=2a m with m odd, we have s‚Č°1(mod2a),s‚Č°0(modm).

If f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. Thus the valid elimination isY=Ps (T)X+YňČ,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís.

The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),for ő∂f=1, ő∂ÓÄ†=1,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and 2s+1‚Č°1(modm), we get gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. So the trivial block nonsingularity proof survives with this convention.With these formulas, the prior finite-tame abelianization conclusions remain consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.2. New point: general Frobenius-lift gauge on the involution directions

Turn 49 generalized the mixed-tame first IA ambiguity: for f even, the nonfree first IA directions are two universal square directions plus directions indexed by involutions of the finite tame quotient. I found a useful gauge refinement of that statement.LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,with e odd and e‚ą£2f‚ąí1. Put f=2a m, m odd, and let

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©,P=őĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a.For f even, write n=f/2. The involutions in őĒe,fareőīj=ŌĄňČjŌÉňČn,j(1+2n)‚Č°0(mode).

Thus their number is

I(e,f)=gcd(e,2n+1)=gcd(e,2f/2+1).

The corresponding mixed degree-two direction isZj=h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,Xhőīj].

Now change the tame Frobenius lift byŌÉňČ‚Ä≤=ŌĄňČkŌÉňČ.

This is still a valid tame Frobenius lift, sinceŌÉňČ‚Ä≤ŌĄňČ(ŌÉňČ‚Ä≤)‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2. But(ŌÉňČ‚Ä≤)n=(ŌĄňČkŌÉňČ)n=ŌĄňČ k(1+2+‚čĮ+2 n‚ąí1)ŌÉňČn=ŌĄňČk(2n‚ąí1)ŌÉňČn.

Therefore the involution index transforms as j‚üľj+k(2f/2‚ąí1)(mode).

Because e‚ą£(2n‚ąí1)(2n+1) and e is odd, the two factors 2n‚ąí1 and 2n+1 are coprime modulo the relevant divisor decomposition. Hence the translations by k(2n‚ąí1) act simply transitively on the allowed set {j‚ąąZ/e:j(1+2n)‚Č°0(mode)}.

So the individual label Z0 is not intrinsic. What is intrinsic before fixing a Frobenius-lift convention is the orbit{Zj:j(1+2f/2)‚Č°0(mode)}.

This generalizes the S3 observation from the prior turns: in (e,f)=(3,2), replacing ŌÉ by ŌĄk ŌÉ cycles the three classes Z0,Z1,Z2. In (e,f)=(5,4), the same gauge action cycles the five involution directions attached to ŌĄjŌÉ2.The same gauge action applies to the higher restricted-power towers from Turn 49:őīj,r (b)=Zj[2r].Thus a future arithmetic comparison in (5,4) should not declare failure if it obtains Z3 while the candidate computation is written as Z0, unless the same tame Frobenius lift has first been fixed. Without such a fixed lift, the correct target is the orbit of five classes, not a named basis vector.

3. Consequence for the remaining proof gap The live word remains unrefuted. The corrected algebra preserves the finite-tame abelianization, sign-kernel, and cup-product consistency checks recorded in the prior log. The new gauge observation narrows what the next comparison should mean:For (e,f)=(5,4), the first IA ambiguity has seven coordinates: two universal square directions and five involution-indexed directions. But the five involution coordinates are only labeled after a Frobenius-lift gauge is chosen. The meaningful arithmetic task is therefore:fix a standard tame degree-20 extension and a specific Frobenius lift;compute the arithmetic class in the two universal square directions and the five involution directions;compare it to the candidate in the same gauge;only then decide whether the candidate‚Äôs filtered action matches arithmetic.This does not prove the presentation. It prevents a likely false negative in the next test and clarifies exactly what data must be matched.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. The stable formulas areDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís.

With these conventions, the prior conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4 remain consistent. New this turn: changing the tame Frobenius lift ŌÉ‚Ü¶ŌĄk ŌÉ acts simply transitively on the involution-indexed first IA directions Zj, via j‚Ü¶j+k(2f/2‚ąí1).

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: For (e,f)=(5,4), fix a concrete tame degree-20 extension and a Frobenius-lift convention. Compute the arithmetic first IA class in the two universal square directions and the five involution-indexed directions, then compare it to the candidate in that same gauge.[BREAKTHROUGH]
